stories.name: add-delete-command-tests
stories.title: Add TestDeleteCommand behavioral tests for handle_delete
stories.description: Add a TestDeleteCommand class to tests/test_ipc_handler.py following the exact pattern of TestAcceptCommand and TestAbortCommand. The class must contain six behavioral tests covering: (1) DELETE adds the index to _deleted_indices, (2) DELETE sends DELETE_ACK with the correct index, (3) DELETE deletes the real temp file from disk using tmp_path, (4) DELETE of an already-deleted index is idempotent (no cleanup, still sends DELETE_ACK), (5) DELETE of a non-existent index is idempotent (no state change, still sends DELETE_ACK), and (6) DELETE then ACCEPT excludes the deleted image from the accept_all call. After adding the tests, run ruff linting to confirm no formatting issues.

includes: test_delete_sends_delete_ack_event (AC-002): asserts DELETE_ACK sent with correct index
test_delete_deletes_temp_file (AC-003): uses tmp_path, asserts real file deleted
test_delete_already_deleted_is_idempotent (AC-004): asserts no-op on double-delete, DELETE_ACK still sent
test_delete_nonexistent_index_is_idempotent (AC-005): asserts no-op for missing index, DELETE_ACK still sent
test_delete_then_accept_excludes_deleted_image (AC-006): asserts accept_all called without deleted image
excludes: Changes to handler.py (handle_delete is already fully implemented)
Index management tests (already covered in test_ipc_handler_index_management.py)

test_fixtures.description: Real temp file created via pytest tmp_path fixture for filesystem deletion assertions
test_fixtures.reason: AC-003 requires asserting an actual file is deleted from disk — a Mock cannot verify filesystem side effects

scenarios.name: delete-removes-file-and-acks
scenarios.description: Construct a MessageHandler with a real BufferedImage whose temp_path points to a real file created by tmp_path. Call handle_delete and verify the file is removed and DELETE_ACK is sent with the correct index.
user_steps: Create a real file at tmp_path / 'img0.png'
Construct BufferedImage with temp_path set to that file
Set handler._image_index_map = {0: buffered_image} and handler._deleted_indices = set()
Assert mock_server.send called once with MessageType.DELETE_ACK and payload['index'] == 0

scenarios.name: delete-then-accept-excludes-deleted
scenarios.description: Set up a handler with two images, delete index 0 via handle_delete, then call handle_accept. Verify that backend.accept_all receives only the image at index 1.
user_steps: Set handler._image_index_map = {0: image1, 1: image2} and handler._deleted_indices = set()
Set handler.backend to a Mock(spec=TextbrushBackend) with accept_all returning [tmp_path / 'out.png']
Assert accept_all was called with [image2] only

stories.notes: All six tests follow the Mock(spec=TextbrushBackend) + mock_server pattern established by TestAcceptCommand and TestAbortCommand. Method names follow the test_{what}_{outcome} convention. The tmp_path fixture is pytest-native and requires no additional dependencies. Run 'python -m pytest tests/test_ipc_handler.py::TestDeleteCommand -v' to verify. Run 'ruff check tests/test_ipc_handler.py' after implementation.

dependency_notes: Single story. No cross-story data flows. The implementation under test (handle_delete in handler.py) is already complete; this story only adds tests.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks are introduced. The tmp_path test fixture is permanent CI infrastructure, not a temporary mock.
